name: Startup Pitch Deck
summary: The canonical investor-pitch arc, drawn from Sequoia and Y Combinator's templates. Optimized for 10–15 minute meetings with VCs and angels. Each beat answers the question an investor will ask next.

beats.name: Company Purpose
beats.description: Open with a single sentence that captures what the company does and why it exists. Should be immediately understandable without industry jargon. Often doubles as the answer to 'so what do you do?'

beats.name: Problem
beats.description: Describe the pain point you solve. Make it concrete with a specific user or customer scenario. The more visceral and recognizable the problem, the stronger the rest of the pitch lands.

beats.name: Solution
beats.description: Show how you solve the problem and why your approach is uniquely better. Lead with the outcome for the customer, then show the mechanism. This is your product story.

beats.name: Why Now
beats.description: Explain the technological, regulatory, behavioral, or market shift that makes this opportunity possible today and impossible five years ago. Investors want to know why this is a window, not just an idea.

beats.name: Market Size
beats.description: Size the opportunity. Top-down (TAM/SAM/SOM) or bottom-up (units × price). Show how big the prize is if you win, and why this market is attractive to a venture-scale outcome.

beats.name: Product
beats.description: Demonstrate the product in action. Screenshots, a short demo, or a walk-through of the core user flow. Show real software solving real problems for real users.

beats.name: Traction
beats.description: Prove momentum. Revenue, users, growth rate, customer logos, retention, engagement — whatever metrics best show the business is working. One credible chart beats five vanity numbers.

beats.name: Team
beats.description: Establish why this team uniquely wins this market. Founder backgrounds, relevant experience, prior outcomes, key advisors. Investors back people first; make the case.

beats.name: Business Model
beats.description: Explain how the company makes money. Pricing, unit economics, sales motion, payback period. Should answer: what does it cost to acquire a customer, and what do they pay you?

beats.name: The Ask
beats.description: State the round size, intended use of funds, and what milestones the capital takes you to. Be specific — investors should leave knowing exactly what you want and what they get.
